
::: {.paper-table}
**Table 29. C.2 Self-regulation and willpower**

| Finding | Published effect | Implication if true | Source |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ego depletion | d =0.62 (meta-analytic) | Every act of self-control drains a finite budget; restraint in the morning predicts indulgence in the afternoon | Hagger et al. [@hagger2010] meta-analysis |
| Glucose restores self-control | substantial | A lemonade rescues willpower; low blood sugar is moral weakness | Gailliot, Baumeister et al. [@baumeister2007] |
| Decision fatigue → judicial sentencing | large swings by time of day | Parole decisions depend heavily on whether the judge has eaten recently | Danziger, Levav & Avnaim-Pesso [@danziger2011] |
| Power posing (hormonal) | T +20%, C −25% | Two minutes of expansive posture substantially alters your endocrine profile | Carney, Cuddy & Yap [@carney2010] |
| Smiling pen → cartoon enjoyment | 0.82 units (original) | The muscles of your face alone substantially determine whether something is funny | Strack, Martin & Stepper [@strack1988] |
:::
